intro: The pivotal question between Stark and A11yWatch is how much of your setup stays portable: Stark carries medium lock-in and A11yWatch carries high lock-in. Stark is design-tool accessibility plugin for Figma, Sketch, and Adobe XD (contrast, vision sim). A11yWatch is automated accessibility monitoring with WCAG rule reports. Pick Stark when the job is design teams catching a11y issues in figma before engineering and you accept medium lock-in; pick A11yWatch when it is engineering teams adding automated a11y checks to ci pipelines and you accept high lock-in. The data we have shows Stark at mostly transparent pricing and docs with average developer experience, and A11yWatch at mostly transparent pricing and docs with average developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Stark is a poor fit for teams wanting automated scans of production web apps, and A11yWatch is a poor fit for legal or compliance teams needing certified wcag audits. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Stark is for design-tool accessibility plugin; A11yWatch is for automated accessibility monitoring; decide based on how portable the setup must stay.
verdict_a: Choose Stark if some ecosystem coupling is acceptable and the work maps to design-tool accessibility plugin for Figma, Sketch, and Adobe XD (contrast, vision sim).
verdict_b: Choose A11yWatch if you can tolerate some setup friction and the work lines up with automated accessibility monitoring with WCAG rule reports.

faq.a: Neither Stark nor A11yWatch publishes entry pricing that lets us compare directly — Stark uses a freemium model and A11yWatch uses a freemium model. Get quotes from both before assuming one is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Stark to A11yWatch?
faq.a: Moving from Stark to A11yWatch is feasible, but A11yWatch is rated high lock-in — expect your integrations and workflows to need rework to match its model. Budget for a parallel-run period rather than a hard cutover.

Both Stark and A11yWatch rate the same on developer experience (3/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is A11yWatch a good alternative to Stark?
faq.a: Yes — A11yWatch is a reasonable alternative to Stark for design teams catching a11y issues in figma before engineering.. The practical differences are freemium-vs-freemium billing and medium-vs-high lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat A11yWatch as a credible swap.
